question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the filter() predicate — t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber() — in subscribeTopic() by subscribing to a topic where sequence numbers wrap around (overflow), causing the filter to incorrectly drop or pass messages and distort the count used in fee/resource accounting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic with a startTime far in the past, causing the historical Flux (topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(filter, true)) to return millions of messages before the live Flux even starts, consuming database bandwidth and server memory far beyond the intended per-subscription budget?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the startTime recalculation in incomingMessages() — startTime = last != null ? last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 : filter.getStartTime() — by subscribing to a topic where the last message has a consensusTimestamp near Long.MAX_VALUE, causing +1 to overflow and produce a negative startTime that bypasses time-based filtering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the endTime assignment in missingMessages() — endTime(current.getConsensusTimestamp()) — by subscribing to a topic where current has a consensusTimestamp of 0, causing the gap-recovery filter to have endTime=0 which may be less than or equal to startTime, triggering unexpected behavior in the retriever?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user cause missingMessages() to log the subscriberId and topicId at INFO level for every gap recovery event by subscribing to a high-gap topic, flooding the logging infrastructure with INFO messages and degrading observability for fee-related audit logging?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isNext()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic where the live listener delivers messages with non-sequential sequence numbers, causing isNext() to return false and triggering missingMessages() for every single message, multiplying the number of retrieve() calls by the number of messages received?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to a value exactly equal to startTime+1 nanosecond, causing the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) predicate to terminate the stream after the first message, but still trigger the full historical retrieve() and live listener setup, wasting resources on setup/teardown overhead?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnSubscribe(s -> subscriberCount.incrementAndGet()) and doFinally(s -> subscriberCount.decrementAndGet()) hooks by rapidly subscribing and cancelling to cause the Gauge metric to fluctuate wildly, misleading autoscaling systems into under-provisioning resources and indirectly affecting fee processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged user send a TopicMessageFilter with a topicId that maps to an account or contract entity (not TOPIC type), causing topicExists() to find the entity but then emit an IllegalArgumentException, and repeat this at high frequency to generate sustained exception-handling overhead in the reactive pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() scheduler used for safetyCheck in subscribeTopic() by opening many subscriptions that all trigger the safety check simultaneously, exhausting the bounded elastic thread pool and starving other reactive operations of scheduler threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic and then have the live listener deliver a message with sequenceNumber = last.getSequenceNumber() (duplicate), causing numMissingMessages = -1 and the duplicate-ignore path to be taken, but still consuming the concatMap processing overhead for every duplicate message in a high-throughput scenario?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a subscriberId containing special characters or very long strings (since it defaults to a random 8-char string but may be overridable), causing log injection or excessive log storage consumption in the finished() and missingMessages() log statements?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user set endTime to a nanosecond value that causes Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, filter.getEndTime()) to throw an exception (e.g., value exceeding Instant's valid range), crashing the isComplete() check and causing the subscription to behave unpredictably in terms of resource lifecycle?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic with limit=1 and startTime far in the past, causing the historical retrieve() to scan through millions of records to find the first matching message, consuming database resources disproportionate to the single-message limit that appears to be a cheap operation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the filter.toBuilder().limit(limit).startTime(startTime).build() call in incomingMessages() to produce a new filter where limit=0 (when filter.hasLimit() is false), causing the downstream topicListener.listen() to receive a filter with no limit and stream all future messages without bound?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the concatWith() operator in subscribeTopic() — historical.concatWith(Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live)) — by subscribing to a topic with a very large historical dataset, causing the historical Flux to never complete (due to backpressure) and blocking the live Flux from ever starting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the DomainUtils.now() call in TopicContext constructor (startTime = DomainUtils.now()) by subscribing at a time when the system clock is skewed, causing isComplete() to make incorrect decisions about subscription termination and keeping subscriptions alive beyond their intended duration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription with no endTime and no limit to a topic that receives a continuous high-volume stream of messages, causing the server to maintain an unbounded in-memory buffer of TopicMessage objects in the reactive pipeline, leading to out-of-memory conditions that affect fee processing for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a topic and exploit the safety check path (current==null) in missingMessages() to trigger a retrieve() call with gapFilter that has no endTime set, causing the retriever to return all messages from startTime to the present and consuming unbounded resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the takeUntilOther(pastEndTime(topicContext)) operator by setting endTime to a value that makes pastEndTime() emit very slowly (30-second intervals), keeping the subscription alive for up to 30 seconds beyond the actual data window and consuming resources during that window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged user cause the AtomicReference last in TopicContext to be set to a TopicMessage with a very large consensusTimestamp via onNext(), which then causes subsequent incomingMessages() calls to compute startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 that overflows, producing a negative startTime that bypasses time-range filtering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the filter predicate — last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber() — in subscribeTopic() by subscribing to a topic where sequence numbers are very large (near Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the comparison to behave unexpectedly after overflow and allowing duplicate messages to pass through?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Gauge metric registered in init() — which reads subscriberCount — by opening and closing subscriptions rapidly to cause the Gauge to report inflated subscriber counts, triggering false autoscaling events that increase infrastructure costs beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user set startTime to the maximum valid value (just before DomainUtils.now()) and endTime to startTime+1, causing the subscription to immediately complete after checking for messages in a 1-nanosecond window, but still incurring the full subscription setup cost (entity lookup, listener registration, metric updates)?
